does: This paper introduces Mole-PAIR, a post-training module that addresses out-of-distribution detection in molecular foundation models by formulating OOD identification as a preference optimization problem. The method learns to rank in-distribution and out-of-distribution molecules through a pairwise learning objective that optimizes AUROC, enabling models to distinguish between confident correct predictions and chemical hallucinations. The approach is model-agnostic and designed as a lightweight plug-and-play module compatible with existing foundation models, evaluated across five real-world molecular datasets to demonstrate improved reliability on OOD samples without requiring expensive retraining.
connects: This work addresses a critical limitation in recent molecular foundation models that build on large-scale pretraining paradigms like those in NLP and vision. It responds to the problem of overconfidence on out-of-distribution data, a long-standing challenge in deep learning that has been approached through uncertainty quantification, calibration, and OOD detection methods. The preference optimization framework connects to recent advances in learning-to-rank and pairwise comparison losses, while the molecular application domain extends prior work on molecular property prediction and validation of machine learning in computational chemistry where reliability is paramount for drug discovery applications.
enables: This work opens directions for more trustworthy deployment of molecular foundation models in high-stakes scientific domains by reducing false-positive predictions in drug discovery and protein design pipelines. The modular approach enables rapid integration with emerging larger foundation models without retraining, supporting downstream applications that require uncertainty awareness. Future work could extend the method to multi-task molecular settings, combine it with active learning to identify which uncertain samples should be experimentally validated, or apply similar preference optimization strategies to other scientific foundation models in materials science or protein structure prediction where OOD reliability is similarly critical.
